seoTitle: Categorize Credit Card Transactions in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn to review, match, split, and categorize downloaded credit card transactions in QuickBooks Online's bank feeds so your books stay accurate.
intro: Downloaded card transactions still need review before they belong on your books. This guide covers how to use QuickBooks Online's bank feeds to match, categorize, and split credit card charges accurately, plus how to automate the process with rules.

steps.heading: Open your bank transactions feed
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Bank Transactions, and select your credit card account to see the For Review queue.

steps.heading: Match existing entries
steps.body: For rows with a green Match indicator, click Match to link the downloaded charge to an existing bill or receipt.

steps.heading: Categorize unmatched charges
steps.body: Click an unmatched row, choose the payee, and select the correct expense category from the dropdown.

steps.heading: Split mixed transactions
steps.body: Use the Split option to divide a single charge across multiple expense categories when a purchase covers more than one thing.

steps.heading: Add and automate
steps.body: Click Add to post the categorized transaction, then use Create rule from this transaction to auto-categorize similar future charges.

faq.question: What does the green Match bar mean?
faq.answer: It means QuickBooks found an existing entry, like a bill you already recorded, that matches the downloaded transaction.

faq.question: Can I split one charge across multiple categories?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Split button on a transaction row to divide the amount across several expense accounts.

faq.question: How do I stop having to categorize the same vendor every time?
faq.answer: Create a bank rule from a categorized transaction so QuickBooks automatically applies the same category to future charges from that vendor.

faq.question: What if I categorize something incorrectly?
faq.answer: You can reopen the transaction from your register and change the category or payee at any time.
